info.title: Chronary API
info.description:
Calendar-as-a-service API for AI agents.

Manage calendars, events, and availability for AI and human agents.
All endpoints require an API key passed as a Bearer token.

**Authentication:** `Authorization: Bearer chr_sk_...` or `Authorization: Bearer chr_ak_...`

**Rate limit:** 10 requests/second per API key. Exceeds return HTTP 429.

**Quotas:** Monthly operation limits by plan — see [Quotas](https://docs.chronary.ai/reference/quotas).

**Agent bootstrap:** Custom extensions prefixed `x-agent-*` describe the self-signup flow for AI agents. These are not part of any registered OpenAPI extension — they are a Chronary convention.

BearerAuth.description: API key with `chr_sk_*` for org-level access or `chr_ak_*` for agent-scoped access. Pass in the Authorization header: `Authorization: Bearer chr_sk_...`

SessionAuth.name: chronary_session
SessionAuth.description: Console session JWT (HS256), issued by sign-in and sent as the `chronary_session` cookie (a Bearer JWT is also accepted). This is NOT a chr_sk_*/chr_ak_* API key — API keys are rejected with 401 on these endpoints.

externalId.description: Provider calendar ID when bridged to an external provider (Phase 3); null otherwise.

provider.description: External provider this calendar is bridged to (google | microsoft | apple); null otherwise.

default_reminders.description: Default reminder offsets (minutes before start) inherited by events that don't set their own. null = use the system default (10 min); [] = no reminders.

reminders.description: Reminder offsets (minutes before start). Each fires an event.reminder webhook and emits a VALARM in the iCal feed. null = inherit the calendar default (then the system default of 10 min); [] = no reminders.

holdExpiresAt.description: When set, the event is a tentative hold that auto-expires at this timestamp. Must be 30 seconds to 15 minutes in the future when creating a hold; the cap exists to keep held slots short enough that they do not block real scheduling for long.

holdPriority.description: Priority for conflict resolution. Higher values pre-empt lower-priority overlapping holds.

recurrenceRule.description: RFC 5545 RRULE subset (no "RRULE:" prefix) when this event is a recurring series master, e.g. "FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=MO,WE;COUNT=12". Expansion is UTC-only. null = one-off event.

recurrenceExdates.description: ISO 8601 starts of individually cancelled occurrences (EXDATE).

recurringEventId.description: Present only on expanded instances (expand=true): the id of the recurring series master this instance belongs to.

originalStartTime.description: Present only on expanded instances: the occurrence start this instance was generated for.

recent_events.description: Up to 3 most recently ended events

upcoming.description: Up to 5 events starting within the next 24 hours

per_agent_busy.description: Busy blocks keyed by agent id. Present only when include_busy=true.

busy.description: Flat busy-block list. Present only when include_busy=true.

consecutiveFailures.description: Circuit-breaker counter: consecutive terminal delivery failures. Reset to 0 on any successful delivery; the endpoint is auto-deactivated at 20 or after 24h of continuous failure.

firstFailureAt.description: Timestamp of the first failure in the current failure streak; null when there is no active streak.

stats.description: All-time delivery counts for this webhook

recurring_events.description: Active recurring event series (DB-counted masters). Deleting a series frees its slot.

limit.description: Plan cap on active agent-scoped keys. `null` means unlimited (Scale).

scoped_keys.description: Active agent-scoped (`chr_ak_*`) keys. `count` is a direct count; `limit` is the plan cap (`null` = unlimited).

holds.description: Temporal-hold lifecycle counters for the current period. Informational — not gated by any plan limit. Funnel identity: `created = confirmed + expired + active`, where `active` is derived (not stored). Counts cover all three end-of-hold paths (TTL expiry, manual release, priority bump).

cross_calendar_queries.description: Availability requests that touched more than one calendar in the current period. Informational — gated separately by the `cross_calendar_availability` capability, not by this counter.

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. Null if no more results.

retention_days.description: Audit-log retention window for the caller plan (days). Null = unlimited (Custom plan).

range_clamped.description: True when the requested `from` was outside the retention window and the server clamped it. UI uses this to render an upgrade hint.

material.description: When `true`, clients should block on re-acceptance.

secret.description: HMAC-SHA256 signing secret — returned once at creation time only. Store it securely.

intent.example: Building an AI scheduling agent for sales teams.
intent.description: "What are you building?" — free-text, capped at 200 chars.

message.example: You're on the list. Check your email for a confirmation.

percent_full.description: Percent of soft-launch slots currently occupied. 0–100.

status.description: `open` when new auto-approvals are accepted; `full` when at capacity.

message.example: The availability endpoint returns empty slots when timezone is UTC+13.

price.description: Recurring monthly amount in the smallest currency unit (e.g. USD cents). `null` for the custom-priced tier.

currency.description: Lowercase ISO-4217 code. `null` for the custom-priced tier.

display_features.description: Marketing copy for UI rendering. Not machine-readable — use `limits` for capability checks.

recommended.description: Hint for UIs to highlight one tier (currently `pro`).

custom_pricing.description: Present and `true` for the custom tier.

contact_url.description: Sales contact URL for the custom-priced tier.

webhook_endpoints.description: Max active webhook subscription endpoints per org.

scoped_keys.description: Max live agent-scoped API keys (`chr_ak_*`) per org.

recurring_events.description: Max active recurring event series per org. Free-plan series must also be bounded (COUNT or UNTIL) and end within 90 days of the series start.

PlanLimits.description: Enforced caps. `null` for the custom-priced tier (negotiated separately).

get.description: Returns the current server status. Does not require authentication.

Returns the catalog of publicly-available plans with pricing, limits, and display metadata. No authentication required; IP-rate-limited. Responses are cacheable (`Cache-Control: public, max-age=300, s-maxage=3600, stale-while-revalidate=86400`) with an `ETag` for 304 conditional requests.

get.summary: Get current Terms of Service version
get.description: Returns the latest ToS manifest entry. No authentication required — intended for CLIs, SDKs, and agent-signup flows that need to read the current version at runtime rather than rely on a build-time constant.

patch.summary: Accept the current Terms of Service
patch.description: Appends an immutable row to `tos_acceptances` (source=`reaccept`) capturing the accepting org, client IP, user-agent, and the ToS document SHA-256, then updates the denormalized `accepted_terms_version` / `accepted_terms_at` columns on the organization. Authenticated via JWT (console session cookie or Bearer token), not an API key.

400.description: Validation error — invalid request body or query parameters

401.description: Missing or invalid console session — these endpoints require a console JWT, not an API key.

409.description: ToS version stale — the submitted `tos_version` does not match `CURRENT_TERMS_VERSION`. Response body includes a `current_version` field on the `error` object.

post.summary: Accept the current Terms of Service (API key)
post.description: Bearer-API-key equivalent of `PATCH /v1/auth/terms`, for SDK / MCP clients that have no console session. Use it to clear a material ToS bump signalled by the `Chronary-Terms-Upgrade-Required` response header. Reaches the same re-acceptance path (appends an immutable `tos_acceptances` row and updates the org). Requires an org-level API key (`chr_sk_*`) — agent-scoped keys are rejected with 403.

400.description: Validation error — invalid request body or query parameters

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

403.description: Forbidden — quota exceeded or operation not permitted

409.description: ToS version stale — the submitted `tos_version` does not match `CURRENT_TERMS_VERSION`. Response body includes a `current_version` field on the `error` object.

get.summary: Export all account data (GDPR Art. 15 + 20 portability)
get.description: Returns a JSON dump of every row this org owns: agents, calendars, events (with decrypted titles + descriptions), iCal subscriptions (with decrypted URL), webhook subscriptions (with decrypted secret), availability rules, scheduling proposals + slots + responses, API key prefixes, ToS acceptance history, and usage/quota counters. Internal infrastructure data (incident_records, scheduling internals, password/OTP/key hashes, claim tokens) is excluded. JWT-authenticated (console session cookie or Bearer token). Rate-limited to 10 exports/hour/org. Response includes `Cache-Control: no-store` and `Content-Disposition: attachment` so the browser triggers a download.

401.description: Missing or invalid console session — these endpoints require a console JWT, not an API key.

429.description: Rate limited — 10 requests per second per key exceeded

post.description: Initiates a claim of an agent-created Chronary account. Requires console JWT auth. Returns the same opaque shape regardless of whether the target email maps to a claimable account — by design, to prevent enumeration. Sends a 6-digit OTP to the target inbox; pair with `POST /v1/auth/claim/verify` within 10 minutes.

400.description: Validation error — invalid request body or query parameters

401.description: Missing or invalid console session — these endpoints require a console JWT, not an API key.

429.description: Rate limited — 10 requests per second per key exceeded

post.description: Verifies the OTP for a pending claim. Requires console JWT auth with `iat` within the last 5 minutes — older sessions return 401 `reauth_required`. On success the claim enters a 24h revocation hold; the original account holder can revoke from the notification email during that window. After 24h a cron promotes the claim and transfers all org-scoped resources.

400.description: Validation error — invalid request body or query parameters

401.description: Missing or invalid console session — these endpoints require a console JWT, not an API key.

post.summary: Revoke a verified claim during the hold window
post.description: Public endpoint — authenticates by the `revocation_token` mailed to the agent-org email at verify time. Allowed only while the claim is in `verified` state and the 24h window is still open. Returns 410 once the window closes.

400.description: Validation error — invalid request body or query parameters

get.description: Returns the developer's pending and historical claims, most recent first. Sanitized — never includes OTP hashes, revocation tokens, or full target emails.

401.description: Missing or invalid console session — these endpoints require a console JWT, not an API key.

get.description: Returns a calendar's events in iCalendar (RFC 5545) format. No authentication required — the token in the URL acts as a capability. Responses are cached for 5 minutes and include an ETag for conditional requests.

schema.description: iCal feed token from the calendar `ical_token` field

post.summary: Create an event in a calendar
post.description: Create a confirmed event, a tentative event, or a temporal hold. When creating a hold (`status: "hold"`), `hold_expires_at` is required and must be between 30 seconds and 15 minutes in the future. Holds auto-release at `hold_expires_at`; promote a hold to a confirmed event with `PUT /v1/events/{id}/confirm` before expiry.

put.description: Promotes an event from `status="hold"` to `status="confirmed"`. The hold must still be active (not past `hold_expires_at`). After confirmation, `event.started` and `event.ended` lifecycle webhooks fire at the scheduled times, and the `event.hold_confirmed` webhook fires immediately.

post.description:
Create an organization and restricted API key by submitting an email and the current terms-of-service version.

Three server-side dedup branches — all return HTTP 200 (or 409 only for stale ToS) to block email enumeration:

- **New email** → returns `org_id` + `api_key`. OTP sent to email.
- **Duplicate unverified-agent signup with current ToS** → opaque response (no `org_id`/`api_key`). OTP rotated and re-sent.
- **Duplicate verified / console-origin org** → opaque response. No email sent.
- **Stale `tos_version`** (agent-origin) → 409 with `current_version` hint.

The returned key is bound to an *unverified* org. It is fully usable for reads and writes within the standard per-resource quotas, but the org cannot graduate from the introductory `free-agent-unverified` plan tier until you call `POST /v1/agent/verify` with the emailed OTP. Verification rotates the org to its normal plan (e.g. `free-agent` or — during the soft-launch window — `pro` via the auto-grant capacity gate).

Rate limit: 5 requests per 60 seconds per client IP.
post.x-agent-hint: Call this first. Returns a restricted API key that works immediately for reads + writes. Follow up with POST /v1/agent/verify once the human reads the OTP.

200.description: Sign-up accepted. New-org payload includes keys; duplicate returns the opaque shape.

400.description: Validation error — invalid request body or query parameters

409.description: Submitted tos_version is out of date. Includes current_version hint.

429.description: Rate limited — 10 requests per second per key exceeded

post.description:
Join the Chronary soft-launch waitlist. Creates a row in the `waitlist` intake table — no
organization is created until the prospect is approved by a founder and clicks the signed
invite link sent to their email.

Idempotent on repeat hits: the same email returns 200 with the existing entry instead of 409.
An active (non-waitlisted) account at the same email returns 409 `email_taken`.

Rate limit: per-IP and per-email via the public rate limiter.

200.description: Email already on the waitlist; idempotent re-hit.

201.description: Waitlist entry created. Confirmation email queued.

400.description: Validation error — invalid request body or query parameters

409.description: An active account already exists for this email, or `tos_version` is stale.

429.description: Rate limited — 10 requests per second per key exceeded

get.description:
Returns the percentage of soft-launch slots currently occupied. Public, unauthenticated,
IP rate-limited. Used by the marketing-site homepage to drive the capacity indicator and
flip the CTA between "Get early access" (open) and "Join waitlist" (full).

Absolute slot counts are intentionally not exposed.

After GA cutover the gauge is meaningless and always returns `{percent_full: 0, status: "open"}`.

429.description: Rate limited — 10 requests per second per key exceeded

get.description:
Validates a signed invite token (purpose=`invite_claim`) and returns the email + expiry
so the console can pre-fill the claim form. Read-only — does not consume the token.
Public, IP+token rate-limited.

200.description: Token is valid and entry is in `approved` state.

400.description: Token is missing or malformed.

409.description: Entry has already been claimed or revoked.

429.description: Rate limited — 10 requests per second per key exceeded

post.description:
Consumes a signed invite token and provisions an active org with `plan=pro`,
`plan_source=soft_launch_invite`, `plan_expires_at=ga_date`. Mints a live + test API key
and transitions the waitlist entry to `claimed`.

OTP is intentionally skipped — token possession (delivered to the email captured at
intake) is the email-ownership proof. Public, IP+token rate-limited.

201.description: Org created. Live + test API keys returned (only chance to see them).

400.description: Token is missing or malformed.

409.description: Email already has an active account, or invite already claimed.

429.description: Rate limited — 10 requests per second per key exceeded

post.description:
Promote a restricted agent key to full access by submitting the 6-digit OTP received via email.

On success the org transitions `status: unverified → verified`, the plan upgrades from `free-agent-unverified` to `free-agent`, and the key immediately gains full write access.

On any failure (wrong OTP / expired / already burned after 10 attempts / unknown org) the response is a generic 400 validation_error — no information is leaked about which precondition failed.
post.x-agent-hint: Submit the 6-digit OTP the human received via email. Upgrades the restricted key to the full plan tier. Returns generic 400 on any failure to prevent enumeration.

400.description: Validation error — invalid request body or query parameters

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

429.description: Rate limited — 10 requests per second per key exceeded

post.description: Structured feedback for bugs, features, or friction. Logged to Chronary for triage — no DB record is created. Rate limited to **25 per day per organization** (UTC day). Available on all plans, including free.

X-Chronary-Plan.description: The current plan of the authenticated organization (free | pro | scale)

400.description: Validation error — invalid request body or query parameters

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

429.description: Daily feedback limit reached

X-Chronary-Plan.description: The current plan of the authenticated organization (free | pro | scale)

Retry-After.description: Seconds until the daily feedback quota resets (next UTC midnight)

400.description: Invalid request

x-agent-bootstrap-script.description: One-liner that performs sign-up, prompts for OTP, verifies, and creates a smoke calendar. Accepts CHRONARY_EMAIL and CHRONARY_OTP env vars for non-interactive flows.
